subtasks.description: Add a 'Clear completed' button element below the filters row in the HTML. Style it as a small danger-style button that is hidden by default (display:none) and only shown when completed tasks exist. Add a `.clear-completed-btn` CSS rule and a `.toast` CSS rule for the undo notification that will appear bottom-center.

Add a `clearCompleted()` function that removes all tasks where `completed === true` from the `tasks` array, calls `saveTasks()`, then calls `render()`. Wire the button's click event to this function. Inside `render()`, show or hide the 'Clear completed' button based on whether `tasks.some(t => t.completed)` is true.

Before clearing, snapshot the current tasks array. After clearing, show a fixed toast element (e.g. 'Completed tasks cleared. Undo') with an Undo button. Clicking Undo restores the snapshot, saves, and re-renders. The toast auto-dismisses after 4 seconds using setTimeout. Clear any pending timeout if Undo is clicked or a new clear is triggered.
Run AI review and prepare human handoff

status_history.message: AI review completed. Awaiting human review and PR decision.

validations: Button hidden by default via .clear-completed-btn { display: none } and shown conditionally in render() via tasks.some(t => t.completed)
clearCompleted() filters tasks array, calls saveTasks(), then render() — permanently removes completed tasks from LocalStorage
render() is called immediately after clearing, updating the task list without a page refresh
Toast element with role='status' aria-live='polite' appears after clear with Undo button; auto-dismisses via 4000ms setTimeout
Undo handler restores undoSnapshot, clears the timeout, hides the toast, saves, and re-renders
toastTimeout is cleared in showToast() before setting a new one, preventing stale dismissal if clear is triggered multiple times
tasks.slice() shallow copy is sufficient for undo since task objects only contain primitive-valued properties
Button placed correctly below filters row; styled as btn-danger btn-sm as specified
